stem: The cubic \( p(x) = x^{3} + ([[A]])x^{2} + ([[B]])x + ([[C]]) \) has three real roots. What is the sum of its roots?
solution: By Vieta's formulas, for \( x^{3} + ([[A]])x^{2} + ([[B]])x + ([[C]]) \) the sum of the roots equals \( -([[A]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This reads the \( x^{2} \)-coefficient directly and forgets that the sum of roots is its negative.
This gives the product of the roots, \( -([[C]]) \), instead of their sum.
This gives the sum of pairwise products of the roots (the \( x \)-coefficient), not the sum.
This reports the constant term rather than the sum of the roots.
This is off by one from the correct sum.
This is off by one from the correct sum.

stem: The cubic \( p(x) = x^{3} + ([[A]])x^{2} + ([[B]])x + ([[C]]) \) has three real roots. What is the product of its roots?
solution: By Vieta's formulas, for a monic cubic \( x^{3} + ([[A]])x^{2} + ([[B]])x + ([[C]]) \), the product of the roots equals \( -([[C]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This reads the constant term directly and forgets that the product of roots is its negative.
This gives the negative of the \( x^{2} \)-coefficient (the sum of roots), not their product.
This gives the sum of pairwise products of the roots, not the product of all three.
This negates the sum of pairwise products, which is unrelated to the product of all three roots.
This is off by one from the correct product.
This is off by one from the correct product.

stem: The cubic \( p(x) = x^{3} + ([[A]])x^{2} + ([[B]])x + ([[C]]) \) has three real roots \( r_1, r_2, r_3 \). What is \( r_1 r_2 + r_1 r_3 + r_2 r_3 \)?
solution: By Vieta's formulas, for a monic cubic \( x^{3} + ([[A]])x^{2} + ([[B]])x + ([[C]]) \), the sum of the pairwise products of the roots equals the \( x \)-coefficient \( [[B]] = [[answer]] \) directly.
distractor_rationales: This gives the sum of all three roots instead of the sum of their pairwise products.
This gives the product of all three roots instead of the sum of their pairwise products.
This reads the \( x^{2} \)-coefficient directly without negating it.
This reads the constant term directly without negating it.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: The quadratic \( p(x) = x^{2} + ([[B]])x + ([[C]]) \) has two real roots \( r_1 \) and \( r_2 \). What is \( r_1^{2} + r_2^{2} \)?
solution: By Vieta's formulas, the sum of the roots is \( -([[B]]) \) and their product is \( [[C]] \). Using \( r_1^{2}+r_2^{2} = (r_1+r_2)^{2} - 2r_1r_2 \), this is \( (-([[B]]))^{2} - 2([[C]]) = [[B]]^{2} - 2[[C]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This adds instead of subtracts twice the product when expanding the square of the sum.
This forgets the \( -2r_1r_2 \) correction entirely.
This forgets the \( (r_1+r_2)^2 \) term and uses only the correction.
This swaps the roles of the sum and product coefficients in the identity.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: The cubic \( p(x) = x^{3} + ([[A]])x^{2} + ([[B]])x + ([[C]]) \) has three nonzero real roots \( r_1, r_2, r_3 \). What is \( \dfrac{1}{r_1} + \dfrac{1}{r_2} + \dfrac{1}{r_3} \)?
solution: By Vieta's formulas, \( r_1r_2+r_1r_3+r_2r_3 = [[B]] \) and \( r_1r_2r_3 = -([[C]]) \). Writing the sum of reciprocals over a common denominator, \( \dfrac{1}{r_1}+\dfrac{1}{r_2}+\dfrac{1}{r_3} = \dfrac{r_1r_2+r_1r_3+r_2r_3}{r_1r_2r_3} = \dfrac{[[B]]}{-([[C]])} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to negate the denominator (the product of the roots is \( -C \), not \( C \)).
This inverts the correct fraction entirely.
This inverts the fraction and also forgets the sign correction.
This multiplies the two Vieta quantities instead of dividing them.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: A quadratic has two real roots whose sum is [[S]] and whose product is [[P]]. If the quadratic is written in monic form \( x^{2} + Bx + C \), what is \( B \)?
solution: For a monic quadratic \( x^{2}+Bx+C \), Vieta's formulas give (sum of roots) \( = -B \). Since the sum of the roots is [[S]], \( B = -([[S]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to negate the given sum.
This reports the product of the roots instead of the (negated) sum.
This negates the product instead of the sum.
This adds the sum and product together, which has no basis in Vieta's formulas.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

solution: Since \( p(x) = (x-r_1)(x-r_2)(x-r_3) \), evaluating at \( x=[[k]] \) gives \( p([[k]]) = ([[k]]-r_1)([[k]]-r_2)([[k]]-r_3) \). Negating each factor flips the sign three times (an odd number), so \( (r_1-[[k]])(r_2-[[k]])(r_3-[[k]]) = -p([[k]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to negate \( p([[k]]) \), missing the odd number of sign flips from reversing each factor.
This negates correctly but drops the constant term \( ([[C]]) \) when evaluating \( p([[k]]) \).
This reports the constant term of \( p \) directly rather than evaluating \( p \) at \( [[k]] \).
This negates the constant term alone rather than evaluating and negating the whole polynomial at \( [[k]] \).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
